Package space.sunqian.fs.data.json

Interface JsonFormatter

All Superinterfaces:
ByteDataFormatter<Object>, CharDataFormatter<Object>
public interface JsonFormatter extends ByteDataFormatter<Object>, CharDataFormatter<Object>
Represents JSON data formatter that formats a given JSON data. By default, it uses CharsKit.defaultCharset() to formats the data in bytes.

Note the JsonFormatter does not support JsonData, using JsonData's own methods (such as CharData.writeTo(Appendable)) or converting the JsonData (such as JsonData.asMap()) to format.

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    static @Nonnull JsonFormatter
    defaultFormatter()
    Returns the default JsonFormatter, which uses ObjectSchemaParser.defaultCachedParser() and ObjectConverter.defaultConverter() to pars and convert objects, and doesn't ignore the properties of which values are null, or null values of map, when formatting.
    default @Nonnull String
    format(@Nullable Object data)
    Formates and writes the given data as a JSON string.
    default byte @Nonnull []
    formatBytes(@Nullable Object data)
    Formates and writes the given data as JSON string to a byte array, using CharsKit.defaultCharset().
    default void
    formatTo(@Nullable Object data, @Nonnull OutputStream out)
    Formates and writes the given data as JSON string to the given output stream, using CharsKit.defaultCharset().
    void
    formatTo(@Nullable Object data, @Nonnull Appendable appender)
    Formates and writes the given data as JSON string to the given appender.
    default void
    formatTo(@Nullable Object data, @Nonnull WritableByteChannel channel)
    Formates and writes the given data as JSON string to the given writable byte channel, using CharsKit.defaultCharset().
    static @Nonnull JsonFormatter
    newFormatter(boolean ignoreNullValue)
    Returns the default JsonFormatter, which uses ObjectSchemaParser.defaultCachedParser() and ObjectConverter.defaultConverter() to pars and convert objects, and does ignore the properties of which values are null, or null values of map, when formatting.
    static @Nonnull JsonFormatter
    newFormatter(@Nonnull ObjectSchemaParser objectParser, @Nonnull ObjectConverter objectConverter, boolean ignoreNullValue)
    Returns a new JsonFormatter with the specified ObjectSchemaParser and ObjectConverter to parse and convert objects.

  • Method Details

    • defaultFormatter

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull JsonFormatter defaultFormatter()
      Returns the default JsonFormatter, which uses ObjectSchemaParser.defaultCachedParser() and ObjectConverter.defaultConverter() to pars and convert objects, and doesn't ignore the properties of which values are null, or null values of map, when formatting.

      The returned formatter will format byte[]/ByteBuffer as Base64 string, and it is the only way to format binary data.

      Returns:
      the default JsonFormatter

    • newFormatter

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull JsonFormatter newFormatter(boolean ignoreNullValue)
      Returns the default JsonFormatter, which uses ObjectSchemaParser.defaultCachedParser() and ObjectConverter.defaultConverter() to pars and convert objects, and does ignore the properties of which values are null, or null values of map, when formatting.

      The returned formatter will format byte[]/ByteBuffer as Base64 string, and it is the only way to format binary data.

      Returns:
      the default JsonFormatter

    • newFormatter

      @Nonnull static @Nonnull JsonFormatter newFormatter(@Nonnull @Nonnull ObjectSchemaParser objectParser, @Nonnull @Nonnull ObjectConverter objectConverter, boolean ignoreNullValue)
      Returns a new JsonFormatter with the specified ObjectSchemaParser and ObjectConverter to parse and convert objects.

      The returned formatter will format byte[]/ByteBuffer as Base64 string, and it is the only way to format binary data.

      Parameters:
      objectParser - the specified ObjectSchemaParser
      objectConverter - the specified ObjectConverter
      ignoreNullValue - whether to ignore the properties of which values are null, or null values of map, when formatting
      Returns:
      a new JsonFormatter with the specified ObjectSchemaParser and ObjectConverter to parse and convert objects

    • formatTo

      default void formatTo(@Nullable @Nullable Object data, @Nonnull @Nonnull OutputStream out) throws DataFormattingException
      Formates and writes the given data as JSON string to the given output stream, using CharsKit.defaultCharset().

      Note for the data of type byte[], ByteBuffer, InputStream or ReadableByteChannel, it will be read and formatted as Base64 string; for the data of type char[], it will be formatted as JSON string rather than JSON array.

      Specified by:
      formatTo in interface ByteDataFormatter<Object>
      Parameters:
      data - the given data to be formatted
      out - the output stream to write to
      Throws:
      DataFormattingException - if any error occurs during formatting

    • formatTo

      default void formatTo(@Nullable @Nullable Object data, @Nonnull @Nonnull WritableByteChannel channel) throws DataFormattingException
      Formates and writes the given data as JSON string to the given writable byte channel, using CharsKit.defaultCharset().

      Note for the data of type byte[], ByteBuffer, InputStream or ReadableByteChannel, it will be read and formatted as Base64 string; for the data of type char[], it will be formatted as JSON string rather than JSON array.

      Specified by:
      formatTo in interface ByteDataFormatter<Object>
      Parameters:
      data - the given data to be formatted
      channel - the output channel to write to
      Throws:
      DataFormattingException - if any error occurs during formatting

    • formatTo

      void formatTo(@Nullable @Nullable Object data, @Nonnull @Nonnull Appendable appender) throws DataFormattingException
      Formates and writes the given data as JSON string to the given appender.

      Note for the data of type byte[], ByteBuffer, InputStream or ReadableByteChannel, it will be read and formatted as Base64 string; for the data of type char[], it will be formatted as JSON string rather than JSON array.

      Specified by:
      formatTo in interface CharDataFormatter<Object>
      Parameters:
      data - the given data to be formatted
      appender - the output appender to write to
      Throws:
      DataFormattingException - if any error occurs during formatting

    • format

      @Nonnull default @Nonnull String format(@Nullable @Nullable Object data) throws DataFormattingException
      Formates and writes the given data as a JSON string.

      Note for the data of type byte[], ByteBuffer, InputStream or ReadableByteChannel, it will be read and formatted as Base64 string; for the data of type char[], it will be formatted as JSON string rather than JSON array.

      Parameters:
      data - the given data to be formatted
      Returns:
      the JSON string
      Throws:
      DataFormattingException - if any error occurs during formatting

    • formatBytes

      default byte @Nonnull [] formatBytes(@Nullable @Nullable Object data) throws DataFormattingException
      Formates and writes the given data as JSON string to a byte array, using CharsKit.defaultCharset().

      Note for the data of type byte[], ByteBuffer, InputStream or ReadableByteChannel, it will be read and formatted as Base64 string; for the data of type char[], it will be formatted as JSON string rather than JSON array.

      Parameters:
      data - the given data to be formatted
      Returns:
      the byte array formatted from the given data
      Throws:
      DataFormattingException - if any error occurs during formatting
